description:
You’re an observability engineer who was just handed a small bundle of Grafana dashboard
definitions (JSON files).  
Your goal is to prepare these dashboards for shipping by performing three batch operations
with **find** and **xargs** (and only the core GNU utilities that come with every common
distribution).

Initial state
1. Directory /home/user/dashboards/ already exists and contains only JSON
   dashboard files.
2. No directory named /home/user/archive/ exists yet.
3. You have normal write permissions inside /home/user/.

Required end-state
1. Create the directory /home/user/archive/.
2. Copy every “*.json” file that exists anywhere under /home/user/dashboards/
   into /home/user/archive/.  The copies must preserve the original base file
   names (e.g., “app_error.json” stays “app_error.json”).  Nothing but *.json
   files may be copied.
3. Produce an audit file named /home/user/archive/dashboard_log.txt with the
   exact format described below.

Exact audit-file format (/home/user/archive/dashboard_log.txt)
• Line 1 must read (case-sensitive, no spaces before or after the “=” sign):
  Count=<NUMBER>
  where <NUMBER> is the total number of JSON files you copied.
• Starting on line 2, list the *base* names (file names only, **not** full paths)
  of every copied JSON file, one per line, **sorted in ascending
  lexicographical order**.  Do not add blank lines, headers, or comments.
• Example template when three dashboards have been copied:
  Count=3
  app_error.json
  latency_overview.json
  system_health.json

Restrictions & hints
• Accomplish the copy and logging tasks with a combination of
  find … -type f -name '*.json' … | xargs … and other single-purpose
  utilities (cp, stat, wc, sort, etc.).  Aim for 2–3 concise command
  invocations.
• Do not hard-code the expected file names or counts; discover them
  programmatically so the workflow adapts automatically if more dashboards
  appear in the source tree.
• Feel free to print console output that helps a human follow your progress,
  but the automated grader will *only* inspect the final contents of
  /home/user/archive/dashboard_log.txt and verify that the copied files are
  present in /home/user/archive/.

Successful completion criteria (what the grader checks)
1. Directory /home/user/archive/ exists.
2. Every JSON file originally found under /home/user/dashboards/ is present
   (with identical base names) in /home/user/archive/.
3. /home/user/archive/dashboard_log.txt exists *and* matches the exact format
   described above.

Remember: your solution will be evaluated by an automated test suite, so the
output file’s formatting and content must be exact.
truth:
Pre-created resources before the agent starts
1. /home/user/dashboards/app_error.json  
   Contents (single line, trailing newline):  
   {"dashboard":"app_error"}
2. /home/user/dashboards/latency_overview.json  
   Contents (single line, trailing newline):  
   {"dashboard":"latency_overview"}
3. /home/user/dashboards/system_health.json  
   Contents (single line, trailing newline):  
   {"dashboard":"system_health"}

Files/directories that MUST exist when the agent finishes
1. /home/user/archive/  (directory)
2. /home/user/archive/app_error.json        – byte-for-byte copy of the source
3. /home/user/archive/latency_overview.json – byte-for-byte copy of the source
4. /home/user/archive/system_health.json    – byte-for-byte copy of the source
5. /home/user/archive/dashboard_log.txt     – exact contents:
   Count=3\n
   app_error.json\n
   latency_overview.json\n
   system_health.json\n

(No trailing blank lines after “system_health.json”. The final newline shown
above is the normal POSIX newline that terminates the last line.)
